meaning: adj.无准备的,即席的,尚未准备好的
examples: Nijay represents a large and growing group of Americans: first-generation college students who enter school unprepared or behind.
She was totally unprepared for his response.
It was a crisis for which she was totally unprepared.
This means that it is unprepared to deal with unexpected circumstances that may require emergency spending.
They are bright and eager, but unprepared to work effectively for a global corporation.
